serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass of orange juice served at a Singapore Chinese restaurant, approximately 240 ml (240 g).

nutrient_source.title: Orange juice, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 169098)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken from USDA SR Legacy entry for raw orange juice, which represents freshly squeezed orange juice as served in restaurant contexts. This is the closest approved-source match for a freshly squeezed orange juice served at a Singapore Chinese restaurant.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Orange juice GI reported as approximately 50 (low GI) in the Foster-Powell et al. 2002 international GI table, a peer-reviewed source. Glycemic load per 240 g serving calculated as (50 × 10.4 × 2.40) / 100 = 12.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content; high potassium content
hypertension.guidance: Orange juice is very low in sodium (1 mg per 100 g) and provides approximately 200 mg potassium per 100 g, which supports healthy blood pressure; however, portion size should be moderated due to natural sugar content.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Orange juice has a GI of approximately 50 and delivers approximately 8.4 g of free sugars per 100 g; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to small servings (120–150 ml) and prefer whole fruit over juice to benefit from dietary fibre.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat; high vitamin C and potassium; free sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Orange juice is very low in saturated fat and provides vitamin C and potassium, which are cardioprotective nutrients; however, regular large-volume consumption should be avoided due to free sugar content that may contribute to elevated triglycerides.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Orange juice (橙汁, chéng zhī) is a beverage made by squeezing or pressing fresh oranges to extract their liquid. In Singapore Chinese restaurant contexts, it is commonly served as a chilled or room-temperature drink, either freshly squeezed or from a commercial concentrate. The juice has a bright, tangy-sweet flavour with a thin, slightly pulpy texture depending on preparation. Nutritionally, orange juice provides approximately 45 kcal per 100 g, with 10.4 g of carbohydrate (predominantly free sugars at 8.4 g), 0.7 g protein, and 0.2 g fat. It is an excellent source of vitamin C (approximately 50 mg per 100 g), a good source of folate (30 mcg DFE per 100 g), and provides meaningful potassium (200 mg per 100 g). The glycemic index is approximately 50 (low GI), but the glycemic load per standard 240 ml serving is approximately 12.5, which is moderate. Clinically, orange juice is suitable for most individuals in moderate portions; those with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake due to free sugar content and the absence of the fibre present in whole fruit. The very low sodium content makes it appropriate for individuals managing hypertension. Orange juice is vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, halal, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a glass of orange juice?
What is the GI of orange juice?
Is orange juice good for blood pressure?
How much sugar is in orange juice?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy for raw orange juice (FDC ID 169098), an approved primary source with exact match to the food type. GI value sourced from Foster-Powell et al. 2002 peer-reviewed international GI table, an approved GI source.
notes: trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; orange juice contains no trans fats by nature (naturally zero). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as orange juice is a plant-derived food containing no vitamin B12. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero in unfortified orange juice; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. The food is listed under cuisine 'Chinese' in the field survey source, reflecting its context as a beverage served at a Singapore Chinese restaurant (See Ya Tonight 今晚有局). Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules as the food is served in a Singapore context. Price range SGD 6.50 preserved from source. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_100g × serving_size_g) / 100 = (50 × 10.4 × 2.40) / 100 = 12.48, rounded to 12.5.
